expectations.text: Applies the three business thresholds correctly (any ONE suffices): annual gross revenue above the $25M threshold (as CPI-adjusted); buying/selling/sharing PI of 100,000+ consumers or households (CPRA raised this from 50,000); or 50%+ of revenue from selling/sharing PI — and concludes the company IS subject via the revenue threshold ($30M > $25M) even though 80,000 < 100,000
expectations.evidence: Lists all three prongs (revenue >$25M, 100,000+ consumers/households, 50%+ revenue from data) and concludes: 'With $30M in annual revenue, you exceed the $25M threshold under prong (1) regardless of how many California consumers you collect data from... You've already met the bar through revenue alone.'

expectations.text: Notes the 'doing business in California' element (for-profit, collects CA residents' PI, determines purposes/means) — being US-based outside California does not avoid the law
expectations.evidence: 'Confirm you're doing business in California (selling to CA residents generally counts) — this seems clearly true given you have CA customers. Note the CPRA also technically requires the business to be for-profit and to determine the purposes/means of processing — standard for an e-commerce company.'

expectations.text: Lists core obligations: notice at collection, privacy policy, consumer request handling (know/delete/correct/opt-out/limit), verifiable request processes, service-provider contracts, non-discrimination
expectations.evidence: Sections 1-7 cover Notice at Collection, Privacy Policy, Consumer Rights (Know/Delete/Correct/Opt-Out/Limit SPI/Non-Discrimination), Consumer Request Mechanisms (verification), and Service Provider/Contractor/Third-Party Contracts.

expectations.text: Mentions the 45-day response deadline for consumer requests with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: 'Respond within 45 days (extendable by another 45 days with notice to the consumer)'

expectations.text: Mentions opt-out of sale/sharing mechanics: 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link and honoring opt-out preference signals such as GPC
expectations.evidence: 'you need a "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link (or use the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal — you're required to honor GPC as a valid opt-out preference signal)'
